1992-05-31_-_Shrimperfest_-_Munchie's,_P: This is the first known perfomance including The Extra Glenns .
1992-07-18_-_Casa_de_Badger_-_Pomona,_CA: "Casa de Badger" is a nickname for Franklin Bruno 's house. It is a reference to " The Badger Song ".
1992-07-22_-_Munchie's_-_Pomona,_CA: Songs 4, 6, 7, and 8 are performed with the Bright Mountain Choir .
Song 2 is performed with the Dark Mountain Boys.
Songs 5 through 8 are performed with Ian Smith on electric guitar. Ian was in John's first band.
1992-12-06_-_Munchie's_-_Pomona,_CA: Songs 8 and 9 were played on John 's Panasonic RX-FT500 boombox.
Songs 10 through 15 were played with Franklin Bruno as the Extra Glenns .
1992-12-12_-_KUCI_Session_-_Irvine,_CA: " One Morning in Northern Italy " was not played as it is usually, according to John .
1992-12-13_-_KSPC_Radio_Session_-_Pomona: According to a Mountain Goats forum thread , this show may also have included the following tracks (which do not appear on the recordings available on MediaFire and on archive.org): Escape to Wild Palm City That Hippolytine Feeling High School Confidential Noche del Guajolote
1992-12-27_-_Xmasfest_-_Munchie's_-_Pomo: "Star Dusting" was incorrectly introduced as " Going to Monaco ".
A recording of this performance of "The Pieman" was included on the Munchies Kinda Xmas tape.
1992-xx-xx_-_Munchie's_-_Pomona,_CA: Comments by John Darnielle on Twitter about a tape of the show he found: "things about this '92 Live at Munchies tape I just found: 1) no banter 2) Chino-heavy crowd goes nuts at "Going to Chino" 3) it's terrible" .
1995-04-12_-_Simplon_-_Groningen,_The_Ne: The first six songs are John solo, and then Rachel joins him.
The encore was " No, I Can't ".
1995-04-19_-_VPRO_Radio_-_Amsterdam,_Net: Allen Callaci of Refrigerator sang "Lonesome Surprise" with John via telephone; this recording is the exact same version that appears on Nine Black Poppies .
Part of a quote by the woman interviewing The Mountain Goats from this session appears before the Nine Black Poppies recording of " Pure Money ".
1995-09-21_-_The_Empty_Bottle_-_Chicago,: This was an Extra Glenns show.
1996-02-25_-_Tower_Musikclub_-_Bremen,_G: John Darnielle played songs 1 through 5 solo. Peter Hughes joined him for songs 6 through 11.
1996-08-30_-_Knitting_Factory_-_New_York: Songs 13 and 14 were performed with Alastair Galbraith.
1996-09-02_-_Tokyo_Rose_-_Charlottesvill: Alastair Galbraith played on songs 16 and 17.
The version of " Black Molly " played at this show was released on " In Release City ".
1996-09-03_-_Fletcher's_-_Baltimore,_MD: Alastair Galbraith played on songs 12 through 16.
" The New Potatoes " was played as a soundcheck.
1996-09-11_-_Argo_-_Denton,_TX: Alastair Galbraith played violin on a few songs, starting with "Grendel's Mother".
John broke a string on "The Only Thing I Know", so he played the electric guitar the rest of the show.
It was when driving from this show to the next one in California that the seeds for All Hail West Texas were planted in John's head.
1996-10-29_-_Studion_-_Stockholm,_Sweden: Peter Hughes played a solo set in the middle of the show, which included songs by Diskothi-Q and Furniture Huschle .
1996-11-09_-_Zwijnzicht_-_Dordrecht,_Net: Peter Hughes joins in from Song 13 onward.
Them & Me and Birdskin were the opening acts.
1997-03-02_-_KJHK-Lawrence_Session_-_Law: A transcript of an interview John Darnielle gave the station is available here .
1997-06-16_-_Club_Laga_-_Pittsburgh,_PA: John planned to play more songs, including " West Country Dream ", " Sept 19th Triple X Love! Love! ", and " Snow Crush Killing Song ".
1997-07-16_-_Capitol_Theater_-_Olympia,_: The recording of song 1 appears on Yoyo A Go Go - Another Live Compilation .
1998-04-12_-_Brownie's_-_New_York_City,_: Alastair Galbraith played on songs 20 and 21.
1998-10-10_-_The_Rocket_Bar_-_St._Louis,: Songs 13 through 16 were performed with Franklin Bruno as The Extra Glenns
1999-01-27_-_Cat's_Cradle_-_Carrboro,_NC: "Commandante" was sung with alternate lyrics in the chorus: “lace up your skates real good, come on over to my neighborhood” and “lace up your skates real tight, be here by six o’clock tonight.”
1999-01-29_-_Mercury_Paw_-_Louisville,_K: Setlist posted on Twitter by user aespenshade on 2018-03-02

2010-12-14_-_Castro_Theater_-_San_Franci: "First things first, though: I am playing two more shows this year. The first is live accompaniment for the film Sir Arne's Treasure, which is a Swedish silent from 1919. I'll be joined by John Vanderslice, Jamie Riotto, and Jason Slota at various points throughout the evening, and I was going to print up a whole program to explain what the whole deal is with what I'm doing but rehearsal took priority and so I'm telling you here instead: to accompany the Swedish film in which bad intentions come to bad ends, I thought I'd play and expand and elaborate on some songs from Sweden . One thing led to another, though, and I ended up synching up a few Hail and Farewell, Gothenburg songs, which is how I got the idea to start excavating a bunch of old Sweden-era notebooks, which is how I ended up finally setting to music a good handful of long-orphaned lyrics from the Sweden cycle: abandoned side-stories, extended soliloquies, muttered curses. So that is what we'll be doing: not all of Sweden, but a version of it, live, as accompaniment for Mauritz Stiller's gorgeous Swedish tale of lost treasures." -- John Darnielle

This was the first in-person live show since the start of the COVID-19 pandemic.
A few hours before the doors opened, John tweeted , "Feels very very good to start writing up a set list for the first time since February 2020"
That night, John tweeted , "To stand on stage and play for you tonight, Asheville, was to experience a homecoming we have yearned for — thank you, from all of us, for this special night"

John intended to play "You or Your Memory" as the fourth song because he did not want to play two new songs in a row, but he had already begun to introduce "The New Hydra Collection" and went with it.
"The Diaz Brothers" was played spontaneously in place of a different song, which was not mentioned.
John intended to wear a black Y&T shirt to the show but decided that it was too tight. Instead, he wore a pink blazer and patterned dark blue button-up.

John described a song that he expects would have been popular if he had written it when NASCAR was at its peak called "Turn Left." It would have taken the narrator from learning how to drive as a child to his death in the third verse, when he would only have 'one turn left' in the style of a country song.
At the end of "Tollund Man," John sang the first verse of "For You" by Electronic.
